gaps: 🧠 LIFECYCLE DIMENSION — Post-DFU state persistence after device restart: No existing test case validates that the firmware version reported after a DFU that fails at >90% persists correctly through a device power cycle (power off → power on → reconnect). Test 64839439 only checks the 'Software Update' button presence. Tests 66363556-66363596 cover language-change-triggered DFU but none verify the firmware version rollback scenario after a device restart post-failure.

🧠 LIFECYCLE DIMENSION — Language reversion after brief use: No test case validates that a language change that appears to succeed (briefly shows new language) then reverts after device restart or after a short period of use. Tests 66363556-66363596 cover language change DFU flows but none test the 'apparent success → silent revert' pattern that is the core of this bug.

🧠 STATE TRIGGER — Failure at >90% completion boundary: No test case specifically targets the critical boundary of >90% completion during DFU. All existing DFU tests assume either success or a clean failure early in the process. The bug's specific trigger (failure in the final <10% of the update) is untested.

🧠 STATE TRIGGER — Intermittent nature / repeatability: No test case includes a 'repeat N times to confirm intermittent behavior' step. The bug description explicitly states the issue is intermittent and requires multiple attempts to reproduce. No existing test has a loop/repeat structure.

🧠 DEVICE ASYMMETRY — Multi-component device firmware rollback: Tests 66363556-66363596 focus on Voyager Free 60+ (earbuds + smart charge case). However, no test validates the scenario where one component (e.g., earbuds) updates successfully while another (e.g., charge case) fails at >90%, causing a partial rollback. The bug could manifest differently on single-component vs. multi-component devices.

🧠 LIFECYCLE DIMENSION — Firmware version check after failed DFU without device restart: Step 7 of the bug checks firmware version immediately after failure (before restart). No existing test validates what the app reports immediately after a failed DFU — whether it shows the old version, a partial version, or incorrectly shows 'up to date'.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-FAIL-001: Firmware version rollback after DFU failure at >90% — device power cycle
steps: Precondition: Device on firmware v1.0.0. New firmware v2.0.0 available.
Navigate to: Device → Software & Updates → Start firmware update.
Allow DFU to progress to >90% (e.g., 93%).
Force DFU failure at >90%: Simulate Bluetooth disconnection OR app force-kill at 93% completion.
Observe: DFU fails. Do NOT restart device yet.
Navigate to: Device → Software & Updates. Record reported firmware version.
Power OFF device → Power ON device → Reconnect via Bluetooth.
Navigate to: Device → Software & Updates. Record reported firmware version.
Repeat steps 2–8 for 3 total attempts to capture intermittent behavior.
generated_testcases.expected: Immediately after failure (Step 6): Firmware version shows v1.0.0 (original). Actual (If Bug): Shows v2.0.0 partially applied or incorrectly shows 'Up to Date'. After power cycle (Step 8): Firmware version MUST show v1.0.0 (original). Actual (If Bug): Shows v2.0.0 (incorrectly indicating success) OR shows a corrupted/invalid version string. The version must not change between Step 6 and Step 8 unless a full successful DFU occurred.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-FAIL-002: Language change DFU — apparent success then silent revert after device restart
steps: Precondition: Device on firmware v1.0.0, language set to English.
Navigate to: Device → Audio Settings → Language → Select 'Deutsch'.
Confirm language change (triggers DFU for language pack).
Observe DFU progress. Allow it to reach >90% completion.
Force DFU failure at >90% (e.g., disconnect Bluetooth).
Observe: Language briefly shows 'Deutsch' on device UI.
Do NOT restart device. Check language on device UI immediately after failure.
Power OFF device → Power ON → Reconnect.
Check language on device UI.
Use device for 2 minutes (play media, adjust volume, answer a call). Re-check language.
Repeat steps 2–10 for 3 total attempts.
generated_testcases.expected: Step 6: Language may briefly show 'Deutsch' (apparent success). Step 7: Immediately after failure, language reverts to 'English'. Step 9: After power cycle, language MUST show 'English'. Step 10: After brief use, language MUST remain 'English'. Actual (If Bug): Language shows 'Deutsch' after restart (Step 9) but silently reverts to 'English' after brief use (Step 10). The revert must be caught within 2 minutes of use.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-FAIL-003: Multi-component device — earbud update succeeds, charge case update fails at >90% causing partial rollback
steps: Precondition: Voyager Free 60+ on firmware v1.0.0 (both earbuds and charge case). New v2.0.0 available.
Navigate to: Device → Software & Updates → Start firmware update.
Monitor individual component progress (earbuds + charge case).
Allow earbud update to complete successfully (100%).
Allow charge case update to reach >90% (e.g., 95%).
Force failure on charge case update at 95% (e.g., remove earbuds from case during update).
Observe: DFU fails. Device restarts.
Navigate to: Device → Software & Updates. Record firmware version for earbuds and charge case.
Power OFF device → Power ON → Reconnect.
Navigate to: Device → Software & Updates. Record firmware version for both components.
Test all audio features: Play media, make a call, test ANC/Transparency.
Repeat steps 2–11 for 3 total attempts.
generated_testcases.expected: Step 8: Earbuds show v2.0.0 (success). Charge case shows v1.0.0 (rolled back). Actual (If Bug): Charge case incorrectly shows v2.0.0 (partial/corrupt version) OR both components show v2.0.0 despite failed charge case update. Step 10: After power cycle, charge case MUST still show v1.0.0. Step 11: Audio features must work normally despite version mismatch. If bug exists, features dependent on charge case firmware may be broken or inconsistent.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-FAIL-004: Immediate state trigger — firmware version check immediately after DFU failure at >90% (no restart)
steps: Precondition: Device on firmware v1.0.0. New firmware v2.0.0 available.
Navigate to: Device → Software & Updates → Start firmware update.
Allow DFU to reach >90% (e.g., 97%).
IMMEDIATELY force-kill the Poly Studio Mobile app (do NOT navigate away, do NOT switch tabs).
Reopen Poly Studio Mobile app.
Navigate to: Device → Software & Updates. Record firmware version.
Check if 'Software Update' banner/button is still present.
Check if device is responsive (play/pause media, volume control).
Do NOT restart device. Repeat steps 2–8 for 3 total attempts.
generated_testcases.expected: Step 6: Firmware version MUST show v1.0.0 (original). Actual (If Bug): Shows v2.0.0 (incorrectly indicating success) OR shows a blank/corrupt version string. Step 7: 'Software Update' banner/button MUST still be present (update available). Actual (If Bug): Banner disappears incorrectly showing 'Up to Date'. Step 8: Device must remain responsive. If bug exists, device may be unresponsive or in a partial update state.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-FAIL-005: Repeatability loop — intermittent DFU failure at >90% across 5 consecutive attempts
steps: Precondition: Device on firmware v1.0.0. New firmware v2.0.0 available.
For attempt = 1 to 5:
Navigate to: Device → Software & Updates → Start firmware update.
Allow DFU to reach >90% completion.
Force DFU failure at >90% (alternate failure methods across attempts: Bluetooth disconnect, app force-kill, device power-off, remove device from range, low battery simulation).
Record: Firmware version shown immediately after failure.
Record: Whether device is responsive or in a stuck state.
Power OFF device → Power ON → Reconnect.
Record: Firmware version after power cycle.
Record: Whether 'Software Update' banner is present after power cycle.
End for.
After 5 attempts, verify cumulative state: Is device still on v1.0.0? Is DFU still offered?
generated_testcases.expected: After all 5 attempts: Device MUST remain on firmware v1.0.0. 'Software Update' banner MUST still be present for v2.0.0. Device must be fully responsive after each power cycle. Actual (If Bug): On any attempt, device shows v2.0.0 after power cycle (false success), OR device becomes unresponsive after repeated failures, OR 'Software Update' banner disappears after N failures incorrectly marking update as complete. Intermittent pattern: Bug may only manifest on 1–2 of 5 attempts.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-FAIL-006: Language change DFU — immediate state trigger without navigation
steps: Precondition: Device on firmware v1.0.0, language set to English.
Navigate to: Device → Audio Settings → Language → Select 'Français'.
Confirm language change (triggers DFU).
Allow DFU to reach >90% completion.
IMMEDIATELY force-kill Poly Studio Mobile app (do NOT navigate away from DFU progress screen).
Reopen app → Reconnect to device.
Check language displayed on device UI.
Navigate to: Device → Audio Settings → Language. Record selected language.
Power OFF device → Power ON → Reconnect.
Check language on device UI again.
Use device for 3 minutes (play media, make call). Re-check language.
generated_testcases.expected: Step 7: Device UI shows 'English' (original language). Actual (If Bug): Shows 'Français' briefly (apparent success). Step 8: App shows 'English' selected. Step 10: After power cycle, language MUST be 'English'. Step 11: After 3 minutes use, language MUST remain 'English'. Actual (If Bug): Language shows 'Français' after restart but reverts to 'English' after brief use — the silent revert pattern.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-FAIL-007: Multi-component asymmetry — charge case update succeeds, earbud update fails at >90%
steps: Precondition: Voyager Free 60+ on firmware v1.0.0 (both components). New v2.0.0 available.
Navigate to: Device → Software & Updates → Start firmware update.
Monitor individual component progress.
Allow charge case update to complete successfully (100%).
Allow earbud update to reach >90% (e.g., 92%).
Force failure on earbud update at 92% (e.g., remove earbuds from Bluetooth range).
Observe: DFU fails. Device restarts.
Navigate to: Device → Software & Updates. Record firmware version for earbuds and charge case.
Test: Place earbuds in charge case → check if charge case touchscreen shows correct firmware info.
Power OFF device → Power ON → Reconnect.
Navigate to: Device → Software & Updates. Record firmware version for both components.
Test all features: Media playback, call, ANC/Transparency, charge case touchscreen.
generated_testcases.expected: Step 8: Charge case shows v2.0.0 (success). Earbuds show v1.0.0 (rolled back). Actual (If Bug): Earbuds incorrectly show v2.0.0 (partial/corrupt) OR charge case incorrectly shows v1.0.0 (unnecessary full rollback). Step 9: Charge case touchscreen must show correct version info. Step 11: After power cycle, earbuds MUST still show v1.0.0. Step 12: Features dependent on earbud firmware (ANC, Transparency, media playback) must work. If bug exists, earbuds may be in a partially updated state causing feature degradation.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-FAIL-008: Deep lifecycle — DFU failure at >90% followed by phone reboot
steps: Precondition: Device on firmware v1.0.0. New firmware v2.0.0 available. Device connected to phone.
Navigate to: Device → Software & Updates → Start firmware update.
Allow DFU to reach >90% (e.g., 94%).
Force DFU failure at 94% (app force-kill).
Do NOT restart device. Do NOT reopen app yet.
Reboot the Android phone completely.
After phone reboot, reopen Poly Studio Mobile app.
Navigate to: Device → Software & Updates. Record firmware version.
Check if device auto-reconnects to phone after phone reboot.
Check if 'Software Update' banner is present.
Power OFF device → Power ON → Reconnect.
Navigate to: Device → Software & Updates. Record firmware version again.
generated_testcases.expected: Step 8: Firmware version shows v1.0.0. Actual (If Bug): Shows v2.0.0 or blank/corrupt. Step 9: Device must auto-reconnect after phone reboot. If bug exists, device may fail to reconnect or show as 'disconnected'. Step 10: 'Software Update' banner MUST be present. Step 12: After device power cycle, firmware MUST remain v1.0.0. The phone reboot must not alter the device firmware state.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-FAIL-009: Post-failure settings persistence after DFU failure at >90%
steps: Precondition: Device on firmware v1.0.0. Set ANC to 80, Transparency Surround to 70, EQ to Bass Boost.
Navigate to: Device → Software & Updates → Start firmware update.
Allow DFU to reach >90% (e.g., 96%).
Force DFU failure at 96% (Bluetooth disconnect).
Reconnect device.
Navigate to: Device → Audio Settings. Record: ANC level, Transparency Surround level, EQ setting.
Power OFF device → Power ON → Reconnect.
Navigate to: Device → Audio Settings. Record: ANC level, Transparency Surround level, EQ setting.
Verify all settings match pre-update values.
generated_testcases.expected: Step 6: ANC = 80, Transparency Surround = 70, EQ = Bass Boost (all preserved). Actual (If Bug): Settings reset to defaults (ANC = 50, Transparency Surround = 50, EQ = Neutral) due to partial firmware state corruption. Step 8: After power cycle, settings MUST remain ANC = 80, Transparency Surround = 70, EQ = Bass Boost. If bug exists, settings may reset after power cycle even if they appeared preserved in Step 6.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-FAIL-010: DFU failure at exact boundary values (90%, 95%, 99%, 100%)
steps: Precondition: Device on firmware v1.0.0. New firmware v2.0.0 available.
For each boundary in [90%, 95%, 99%, 100%]:
Navigate to: Device → Software & Updates → Start firmware update.
Allow DFU to reach exactly {boundary}%.
Force DFU failure at {boundary}% (use consistent method: Bluetooth disconnect).
Record: Firmware version immediately after failure.
Power OFF device → Power ON → Reconnect.
Record: Firmware version after power cycle.
Record: Whether device is fully responsive.
Record: Whether 'Software Update' banner is present.
Reset device to v1.0.0 before next boundary test.
End for.
generated_testcases.expected: For 90%, 95%, 99%: Firmware MUST show v1.0.0 after failure and after power cycle. Device must be responsive. 'Software Update' banner must be present. For 100%: Firmware MUST show v2.0.0 (success). Actual (If Bug): At 99%, device incorrectly shows v2.0.0 after power cycle (false success) OR at 100% device shows v1.0.0 (false failure). The 99% boundary is the highest risk for the 'almost complete' false success pattern.
